---
title: "reticulated shiny rmd"
runtime: shiny
output: html_document
---

**HOW TO RUN THIS SHINY APP IN POSITRON:**

Run this command in your R console (from the workshop root directory):

```r
rmarkdown::run("part1/reticulated-shiny-rmd-py2/index.Rmd")
```

**Do NOT try to render/knit this file** - it needs to run as a Shiny app server.

---

This uses Python (via reticulate) to generate some data, and Shiny to serve a pretty, interactive plot of it.

```{r, echo = FALSE}
# Configure Python for reticulate in Positron
Sys.setenv(RETICULATE_PYTHON = "/opt/python/3.12.3/bin/python3")

library(shiny)
library(reticulate)

source_python('server.py')

sliderInput("bins", "Number of bins:", min = 1, max = 50, value = 30)

renderPlot({
  x <- py$data
  bins <- seq(min(x), max(x), length.out = input$bins + 1)

  # draw the histogram with the specified number of bins
  hist(x, breaks = bins, col = 'darkgray', border = 'white')
})
```
